info.title: Lighthouse
info.description: Knowledge base for AI agents. Public retrieval over HTTP (MCP wrapper on top), proposal pipeline behind a shared API key.

get.description: All importer types the registry knows about.

schema.title: Response List Types Admin Importers Types Get

post.description:
Probe the source with provided creds and list available items.

No DB writes — secrets stay in memory only. The wizard sends them
again on save (where they get encrypted).

schema.title: Response List Route Admin Importers  Get

post.description:
Idempotently provision the per-workspace S3 importer for the
caller's workspace (from ``X-Workspace``). Safe to call on every
workspace setup — repeat calls return the existing importer.

Kick off a run in the background. Returns immediately with the
importer id; poll `/{id}/runs` for the actual run row's progress.

We instantiate the importer up-front (without running it) so a
missing optional-dep package fails the request with a 422 +
pip-install hint instead of letting the background task swallow
the error into the run row.

schema.title: Response Runs Route Admin Importers  Importer Id  Runs Get

get.description: All importer types the registry knows about.

schema.title: Response List Types V1 Importers Types Get

post.description:
Probe the source with provided creds and list available items.

No DB writes — secrets stay in memory only. The wizard sends them
again on save (where they get encrypted).

schema.title: Response List Route V1 Importers  Get

post.description:
Idempotently provision the per-workspace S3 importer for the
caller's workspace (from ``X-Workspace``). Safe to call on every
workspace setup — repeat calls return the existing importer.

Kick off a run in the background. Returns immediately with the
importer id; poll `/{id}/runs` for the actual run row's progress.

We instantiate the importer up-front (without running it) so a
missing optional-dep package fails the request with a 422 +
pip-install hint instead of letting the background task swallow
the error into the run row.

schema.title: Response Runs Route V1 Importers  Importer Id  Runs Get

get.description:
Roll-up counters over the chunks table, scoped to the caller's
workspace. Cheap — one indexed scan.

Per-source roll-up, scoped to the caller's workspace. `order=chunks`
ranks by chunk count (largest sources first); `order=recent` by most
recent ingest.

schema.title: Response Sources V1 Corpus Sources Get
Response List All V1 Webhooks  Get
Response Update V1 Webhooks  Webhook Id  Patch
Response Deliveries V1 Webhooks  Webhook Id  Deliveries Get

post.description:
Reset a delivery row to `pending` + next_attempt_at=NOW. The
worker picks it up on the next tick.

schema.title: Response Redeliver V1 Webhooks  Webhook Id  Deliveries  Delivery Id  Redeliver Post

post.description:
Bulk-requeue every `dead` delivery of this webhook (attempts
exhausted). Resets the retry counter so the worker walks the full
backoff curve again — use after fixing the receiver.

schema.title: Response Requeue Dead V1 Webhooks  Webhook Id  Deliveries Requeue Dead Post

post.description:
Enqueue a synthetic `ping` event for this webhook only.

Useful for first-time setup — verifies URL + signing + receiver
parsing without waiting for a real importer run.

schema.title: Response Test V1 Webhooks  Webhook Id  Test Post
Response Top Queries V1 Analytics Top Queries Get

get.description:
Zero-hit query clusters — the corpus' coverage gaps. Resolved /
ignored clusters are hidden unless ``include_resolved`` is set, so
triaged gaps stop cluttering the dashboard.

schema.title: Response Gaps V1 Analytics Gaps Get

post.description:
Garbage-collect triage state for clusters nobody asks anymore.

Deletes ``coverage_gap_status`` rows whose query has no query_log
entry in the trailing window — including orphans created by triaging
a never-logged string. Triage state for live clusters is untouched.

schema.title: Response Prune Gap Statuses V1 Analytics Gaps Prune Post

get.description:
References per source — how often each indexed source actually
shows up in search results. Sources with many chunks but no
references are dead weight; heavily-referenced ones deserve more
ingest attention.

schema.title: Response Source Usage V1 Analytics Source Usage Get
Response List Keys V1 Keys  Get

delete.description:
Soft-revoke: the key stops authenticating immediately but the
row (and query_log attribution) is kept.

get.description:
The registry — registered workspaces with their auth policy.
(Tenants that never registered still work; they just have no row
here and default to the instance-wide policy.)

schema.title: Response List Workspaces V1 Workspaces  Get

content.title: Content
content.description: The proposed fact or correction, in natural language.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.description: Sources backing the claim — URLs, logs, version refs.

submitted_by.title: Submitted By
submitted_by.description: Free-form attribution tag — stored verbatim, never trusted.

SearchHit.title: SearchHit
SearchHit.description:
One chunk returned by ``/search``.

``summary`` is the chunk's heading + snippet; ``episode_ids`` carries
the chunk uuid — feed it to ``/fetch_source`` for the full text.
``source_node_id`` / ``target_node_id`` / ``valid_until`` are retained
for wire compatibility but are always null (flat-RAG has no entity
layer).
